title: Monnina revoluta
taxonomy: Monnina revoluta belongs to the family Polygalaceae and is a species of flowering plant. The genus Monnina is part of the order Polygalales. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other plants. Further classification within the Polygalaceae family places Monnina revoluta in the tribe Polygaleae. The specific classification of Monnina revoluta can be represented as Polygalaceae, Polygaleae, Monnina.

intro: Monnina revoluta is a plant species belonging to the family Polygalaceae, commonly known as the "milkwort" due to its milky sap. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and protect against oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of Monnina revoluta include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
